title: Aether — a language in your browser
description: A from-scratch ML-family programming language with Hindley–Milner inference, a KIND SYSTEM with HIGHER-KINDED TYPES — genuine Functor/Applicative/Monad classes with SUPERCLASSES (dictionary-passing + superclass projection) — and TWO backends: a bytecode stack VM and a JavaScript code generator that matches the VM byte-for-byte. Now with DERIVING: a `deriving (Eq, Ord, Show, Enum, Bounded, Functor, Foldable)` clause makes the compiler synthesise whole type-class instances from a data type's shape — including position-aware `deriving Functor` and `deriving Foldable` that write fmap/foldr by walking a type's last parameter (recursing through itself, lists and tuples) — as pure parse-time desugaring into ordinary instances, so both backends inherited it with zero changes and inference infers each instance's context. Plus property-based testing that generates random inputs from inferred types and shrinks failures, monadic do-notation that resolves through the real Monad class, list comprehensions, an interactive HM derivation tree, a time-travel debugger, and turtle graphics.
